title: Red Hat Security Advisory: kernel security update
overview:
An update for kernel is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 Extended Lifecycle Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: kernel security update

An update for kernel is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 Extended Lifecycle Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for kernel is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 Extended Lifecycle Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

CVE-2025-39898: No description is available for this CVE.

CVE-2025-39971: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

i40e: fix idx validation in config queues msg

Ensure idx is within range of active/initialized TCs when iterating over
vf->ch[idx] in i40e_vc_config_queues_msg().

CVE-2025-40248: A flaw was found in the Linux kernel's `vsock` component. This vulnerability occurs when a `connect()` operation on an already established socket is interrupted by a signal or timeout, causing the system to mishandle the socket's state. This incorrect handling can lead to a race condition, potentially resulting in memory corruption, such as a use-after-free or null-pointer dereference. A local attacker could exploit this to cause a denial of service or potentially escalate privileges.
